topbar.title: AmImUm Herbal Store Dashboard
topbar.subtitle: This area follows the internal access matrix: shared access for Admin and Owner, owner-only for sensitive actions.
topbar.languageIntro: Choose the dashboard language that best supports your internal workflow.

roleCard.title: Internal Access Layer
roleCard.desc: Admins can access overview, orders, and payments. Owners get additional access to user management and sensitive areas.

nav.catalogDesc: Submit new products and monitor catalog structure
nav.cashierDesc: Internal cashier transactions and fast checkout
nav.inventoryMonitorDesc: Stock monitoring + movement history in one page
nav.usersDesc: Manage internal operations and customer accounts

login.title: Sign in to the dashboard
login.subtitle: Enter your internal account email and password to access the AmImUm Herbal Dashboard.
login.languageIntro: Available in Bahasa Indonesia and English to support internal team operations.
login.sessionNotice: Your internal session will automatically expire after 4 hours of inactivity
login.footerNote: Use an internal account with admin or owner role to access the dashboard. For security, inactive sessions will require sign-in again.

ordersPage.title: Orders Management
ordersPage.subtitle: A monitoring area for admins and owners so daily operations are easier to supervise. The current focus is still list monitoring, not the full detail workflow yet.

summary.inFulfillmentHelper: Orders are being processed or shipped
summary.visibleGrossHelper: Total order value under the current filter

ordersPage.searchPlaceholder: Search by order id, customer, or status...

table.error: Failed to load orders data. Please refresh the page or check the admin orders API connection.
table.empty: No orders matched the current filter.

ordersPage.roleDescription: The orders page is prepared for daily monitoring by admins and owners. Its focus is order visibility, fulfillment status, total transaction value, and quick filtering based on active backend statuses.

paymentsPage.title: Payments Monitoring
paymentsPage.subtitle: A transaction monitoring area for admins and owners so payment statuses are easier to supervise and audit. The initial focus remains monitoring and quick transaction inspection.
paymentsPage.grossDescription: This is the total gross amount for currently visible transactions based on the active filter, helping admins and owners quickly read the payment value being monitored.
paymentsPage.searchPlaceholder: Search by order, transaction, customer, or status...

table.error: Failed to load payments data. Please refresh the page or check the admin payments API connection.
table.empty: No payments matched the current filter.

paymentsPage.roleDescription: The payments page is prepared for transaction monitoring and payment status auditing by admins and owners. Its main focus is reading payment flow health, settlement status, and fraud anomalies quickly.

orderDetailPage.title: Order Detail
orderDetailPage.subtitle: Admin order detail to inspect item structure, shipping, and overall order context more completely.
orderDetailPage.loadError: Failed to load order detail. Please return to the orders page and try again from there.
orderDetailPage.updateDescription: Use this only for internal admin workflow. Make sure the status change matches the real operational order state.
orderDetailPage.selectStatusError: Please choose a new status first.
orderDetailPage.itemsSubtitle: List of items stored in this order.
orderDetailPage.emptyItems: This order has no items to display.
orderDetailPage.shippingSubtitle: Summary of the address and courier stored in this order.
orderDetailPage.emptyShipping: No shipping snapshot is available for this order. This usually happens for pickup orders or when shipment data is not available yet.

variantsPage.title: Variant / Pack Type Management
variantsPage.subtitle: A shared internal module for admins and owners to manage variants after products are created, following the pack_types to products table relationship.

messages.createError: Failed to create a new variant.
messages.invalidUpdateId: This variant does not have a valid id for update.
messages.invalidImageId: This variant does not have a valid id for image upload.
messages.selectImage: Please choose an image file first.

form.title: Variant submit form
form.subtitle: Connected to the pack_types table, with product_id as a foreign key to the products table.
form.expirationPlaceholder: 12/25/2026 or the format used by the backend
form.helper: Variant creation is active through POST /type/create, variant detail update is active through PUT /type/:type_id, and image flow has started being connected to PUT /type/image/:type_id. Variants are treated as the actual sellable unit with their own price.

matrix.title: Endpoint matrix alignment
matrix.sharedInternal: Shared internal: admins and owners may access this area because the backend uses admin_access_required.
matrix.relationFlow: DB relation flow in use: products.id to pack_types.product_id

actions.title: Operational action panels
actions.subtitle: Quick panels to edit stock/discount and upload variant images.
actions.updateEmpty: Choose a variant from the table below to edit pack name, variant, stock, price, discount, and expiration.
actions.uploadEmpty: Choose a variant from the table below to upload a new image.
actions.deleteFlow: The delete flow is still disabled in the UI to keep rollout safe.

table.title: Existing variants
table.subtitle: Monitor the existing variant structure before adding a new pack type.
table.searchPlaceholder: Search product, variant name, or pack type...
table.empty: No variants matched the current search. Try resetting the search or using another keyword.

catalogPage.title: Catalog Management
catalogPage.subtitle: A shared internal module for admins and owners to manage new product submission based on the established database relationships.

errors.createFailed: Failed to create a new product.

productEditPage.title: Edit Product
productEditPage.subtitle: A dedicated product edit page so submit and update flows do not mix, while maximizing the product endpoints already available in the backend.
productEditPage.loadError: Failed to load product data. Please return to the catalog page and try again.

validation.required: Name, info, description, and instructions are required.

productionPage.title: Production / Brand Management
productionPage.subtitle: A shared internal module to manage brands/productions that serve as the source relationship for the product catalog. The category in this flow is the product-layer category, not the article category.

errors.selectCategory: Please choose a product category first.
errors.createFailed: Failed to create a new brand / production.

productionEditPage.title: Edit Brand / Production
productionEditPage.subtitle: The edit CTA now goes directly to a dedicated edit page so the production flow stays focused and does not mix with the create form.
productionEditPage.loadError: Failed to load production data. Please return to the production page and try again.

validation.required: Production name and description are required.

paymentDetailPage.title: Payment Detail
paymentDetailPage.subtitle: Payment transaction detail to help admins and owners read the payment context more completely.
paymentDetailPage.loadError: Failed to load payment detail. Please return to the payments page and try again from there.
paymentDetailPage.transactionHealthSubtitle: Summary of transaction status and fraud monitoring.
paymentDetailPage.fraudWarning: This transaction needs attention because the fraud status is not accept. Cross-check it with the Midtrans response and related order status.
paymentDetailPage.fraudSafe: No visible fraud anomaly is currently detected in this transaction data.
paymentDetailPage.timelineSubtitle: Creation time and latest update time of the transaction.

ordersPageUnified.title: Orders & Payments
ordersPageUnified.subtitle: A unified page to monitor store transactions.

overview.title: Dashboard Overview
overview.subtitle: Monitor your internal business metrics and latest transactions.
overview.orderStatusDesc: Status distribution from live backend summary
